facts.text: Jacob City’s historic water tower, a landmark for the town, dates back to the early 1800s and still stands as a symbol of its legacy community.
The town’s roots stretch back to the early 1800s, making Jacob City one of the oldest settlements in Jackson County.
For generations, Jacob City has hosted barbershops, beauty stores, and small cafés that have become the heart of its social life.
If you need building permits or code enforcement, the Jacob City town hall is the first place to call before reaching out to Jackson County officials.
There is no passport office in Jacob City; residents must travel to nearby county courthouses or post offices for passport services.
Jacob City’s name honors its founder, Jacob, who established the settlement in the early 1800s and helped shape the region’s early development.
